claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific isolation with boundary-controlled tool profiles, but cross-provider model leakage detection requires additional verification.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes provider and model settings, clears claude-specific flags for claude provider, and normalizes tool profiles to boundary/research/full modes.
evidence.why: This is the core provider configuration logic that prevents cross-provider flag leakage in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: When provider is claude, agent_tool_profile is normalized to boundary/research/full, settings/tools flags are cleared, and session persistence is disabled.
evidence.why: Evidence that claude provider gets clean configuration without inheriting generic tools settings.

evidence.summary: Explicit model clearing logic for codex provider: if provider is codex and model contains claude, the inherited model is cleared.
evidence.why: Evidence of attempted mitigation for cross-provider model leakage between claude and codex.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific CLI invocations with isolated argument lists and no cross-contamination.
evidence.why: Shows that each provider gets its own isolated invocation logic without sharing flags.

evidence.summary: Claude provider invocation uses -p flag, optional --model, --permission-mode, and settings paths, but does not inherit codex sandbox flags.
evidence.why: Evidence that claude and codex providers have disjoint CLI contracts.

evidence.summary: Codex provider invocation uses exec, --sandbox, -c for approval policy, and --output-last-message, with separate codex_model handling.
evidence.why: Evidence that codex has its own sandbox and approval system separate from claude.

evidence.summary: PROVIDER_ALIASES maps anthropic→claude, openai→codex, and shell/local→custom for normalization.
evidence.why: Evidence that provider normalization exists and prevents alias-based confusion.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function normalizes provider strings and returns null for auto, defaulting to claude.
evidence.why: Evidence of explicit provider normalization logic.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots detect file mutations using git ls-files and recursive file walking with sha256 digests.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary enforcement can detect project mutations during evidence dispatch.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures before/after boundary snapshots, diffs them, and adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for changes.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations block PRD readiness after agent execution.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines default_mode as dry_run, requires explicit authorization, writes_business_code as false, and agent_instruction as boundary_controlled.
evidence.why: Evidence that evidence dispatch is designed as read-only by default with explicit authorization gates.

evidence.summary: Test codex_dispatch_does_not_inherit_a_claude_default_model verifies codex provider clears claude model inheritance.
evidence.why: Test evidence that cross-provider model leakage is a known concern and is mitigated.

evidence.summary: Test claude_dispatch_does_not_pass_tool_restriction_flags verifies claude provider runs without tool flags.
evidence.why: Test evidence that claude evidence mode intentionally avoids tool restrictions.

evidence.summary: Test claude_full_tool_profile_requires_explicit_authorization verifies full tool profile requires allowFullAgentTools flag.
evidence.why: Test evidence that full tool access requires explicit user authorization.

evidence.summary: Test boundary_blocks_project_file_mutation verifies project mutations trigger BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary enforcement detects and blocks project mutations.

evidence.summary: Test git-backed_boundary_detects_untracked_project_mutations verifies boundary detection works with git and gitignored files.
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary detection handles git-tracked and gitignored files correctly.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier protocols with writes_code=false.
evidence.why: Evidence that evidence agents are contractually read-only.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions that include boundary rules, scope requirements, and JSON output format.
evidence.why: Evidence that agents are explicitly instructed about boundary constraints and evidence format.

assumptions: The model clearing logic for codex (line 447-449) suggests cross-provider model leakage is a known risk, but the implementation only clears claude-prefixed models and does not handle other inheritance patterns.
Claude evidence mode clears tool restriction flags intentionally (line 456-462), but it's unclear if this is for isolation or to rely on harness boundary enforcement.
The boundary snapshot mechanism detects file mutations but does not prevent provider-side tool invocation within allowed artifact roots.
Provider normalization happens at multiple layers (CLI input, executionConfig, buildProviderInvocation), but there is no single source of truth for provider contract validation.
risks: If a provider inherits model settings from config.yaml before provider-specific clearing logic runs, cross-provider model leakage could still occur.
If tool profile normalization fails or is bypassed, providers could receive tool capabilities beyond the intended boundary/research/full modes.
If custom provider commands are not properly isolated, they could write outside the allowed artifact roots without triggering boundary violations.
If git ls-files is not available or git repo is corrupted, boundary snapshot falls back to filesystem walk which may miss untracked changes or have different exclusion behavior.
missing: Evidence that provider-specific configuration clearing happens before CLI invocation construction, not after.
Evidence that custom provider commands are subjected to the same isolation requirements as claude/codex providers.
Evidence that tool profile normalization is applied consistently across all provider dispatch paths, not just evidence dispatch.
Evidence that boundary violations are detected in real-time during agent execution, not just post-hoc via snapshot diff.
External research on claude vs codex tool capability contracts to verify that evidence mode preserves full tool capability under the harness boundary.

result.notes: Provider dispatch implementation shows clear isolation mechanisms with provider-specific CLI contracts, tool profile boundary control, and mutation detection. Test coverage validates critical paths. Assumptions and missing items represent verification gaps but not implementation defects. Proceed with cross-check to validate high-risk claims about cross-provider leakage and boundary enforcement completeness.
